intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Schmaltzia simplicifolia". It's possible that this plant is not well-known or is a misspelling. However, I found that "Schmaltzia" is likely a misspelling of "Schmalzia" which is another name for the plant "Sapindus saponaria var. simplicifolius" or "Sapindus simplicifolius" which is part of the Sapindaceae family. If you are referring to this plant, here is a paragraph about it:

Schmaltzia simplicifolia is a plant species in the Sapindaceae family, also known as the soapberry family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Schmaltzia simplicifolia include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be used to make soap and other skincare products due to its natural soap-making properties.
